rules.title: Conditional Tense Formation - Basic Structure
rules.text: Slovak conditional is formed using the past participle (l-participle) + conditional auxiliary 'by'. The participle agrees with gender and number: -l (masculine singular), -la (feminine singular), -lo (neuter singular), -li (masculine plural), -ly (feminine/neuter plural). The conditional particle 'by' changes form based on person.
examples: robil by som – I would do (masculine)
robila by som – I would do (feminine)
robili by sme – we would do

rules.title: Conditional Auxiliary 'by' - Forms
rules.text: The conditional particle 'by' combines with present tense forms of 'byť' (to be). Word order: participle + by + personal ending.
examples: Ja by som – I would
Ty by si – You would
On/Ona by – He/She would
My by sme – We would

rules.title: Conditional Conjugation - 'robiť' (to do)
rules.text: Full conjugation table for 'robiť' in conditional. Word order: participle + by + personal ending.
examples: Robil by som to – I would do it (masc.)
Robila by si to? – Would you do it? (fem.)
Robili by sme to – We would do it

rules.title: Conditional Conjugation - 'napísať' (to write - perfective)
rules.text: Perfective verbs in conditional describe completed hypothetical actions.
examples: Napísal by som list – I would write a letter (completed)
Napísala by si email – You would write an email (completed)
Napísali by sme knihu – We would write a book (completed)

rules.title: Word Order with 'by' - Standard Sentences
rules.text: Standard word order: Subject + Participle + by + Personal Ending. The particle 'by' comes immediately after the participle and before the personal ending (som, si, sme, ste). In 3rd person, only 'by' is used with no personal ending.
examples: Ja robil by som – I would do (masc.)
Ty robila by si – You would do (fem.)
On robil by – He would do
My robili by sme – We would do

rules.title: Word Order with 'by' - Questions and Emphasis
rules.text: In questions or for emphasis, 'by' can move to second position in the sentence (after the first stressed element). The personal ending stays with 'by'.
examples: Robil by som to – I would do it (statement)
By som to robil? – Would I do it? (question, emphasis on 'by')
Ty by si to robila? – Would you do it? (question)
Kedy by si to robil? – When would you do it?

rules.title: Conditional of 'byť' (to be)
rules.text: The verb 'byť' in conditional uses 'bol/bola/bolo/boli' + by + personal ending.
examples: Bol by som šťastný – I would be happy (masc.)
Bola by som unavená – I would be tired (fem.)
Boli by sme doma – We would be at home

rules.title: Reflexive Verbs in Conditional - Word Order
rules.text: CRITICAL WORD ORDER: Participle + reflexive particle (sa/si) + by + personal ending. The reflexive particle comes BETWEEN the participle and 'by', NOT after the personal ending.
examples: Učil sa by som slovenčinu – I would learn Slovak (masc.)
Učila sa by si viac – You would learn more (fem.)
Učili sa by sme spolu – We would learn together

rules.title: Reflexive Conjugation Table - 'učiť sa' (to learn)
rules.text: Full conjugation showing reflexive particle placement: participle + sa/si + by + personal ending.
examples: Učil sa by som každý deň – I would learn every day
Umyl sa by si ráno – You would wash in the morning
Obliekli sa by sme rýchlo – We would get dressed quickly

rules.title: Reflexive Word Order - Alternative Positions
rules.text: In questions or with emphasis, 'by + personal ending' can move to second position, but the reflexive particle ALWAYS stays immediately after the participle.
examples: Učil sa by som – I would learn (standard)
By som sa učil – I would learn (emphasis on 'by', but 'sa' stays with participle)
Umyl sa by si? – Would you wash yourself?
By si sa umyl? – Would you wash yourself? (emphasis)

rules.title: Negation in Conditional
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the participle. The word order with 'by' remains the same: ne-participle + by + personal ending.
examples: Nerobil by som to – I wouldn't do it
Nevidela by si ma – You wouldn't see me (fem.)
Nepracovali by sme – We wouldn't work

rules.title: Negation with Reflexives
rules.text: Add 'ne-' prefix to the participle. Word order: ne-participle + reflexive particle + by + personal ending.
examples: Neučil sa by som – I wouldn't learn (masc.)
Neumyla sa by si – You wouldn't wash yourself (fem.)
Neobliekli sa by sme – We wouldn't get dressed

rules.title: Aspect in Conditional - Imperfective
rules.text: Imperfective verbs in conditional describe ongoing or repeated hypothetical actions without focus on completion.
examples: Čítal by som knihu – I would be reading a book (ongoing)
Pracoval by každý deň – He would work every day (habitual)
Učili by sme sa slovenčinu – We would be learning Slovak (process)

rules.title: Aspect in Conditional - Perfective
rules.text: Perfective verbs in conditional describe completed hypothetical actions with a clear result.
examples: Prečítal by som knihu – I would read the book (finish it)
Napísal by list – He would write a letter (complete it)
Kúpili by sme auto – We would buy a car (completed action)

rules.title: Aspect Pairs in Conditional
rules.text: Use imperfective for hypothetical process/duration, perfective for hypothetical completion.
examples: Písal by som (imp.) – I would be writing
Napísal by som (perf.) – I would write/finish writing
Jedol by som (imp.) – I would be eating
Zjedol by som (perf.) – I would eat it all up

rules.title: Irregular Conditional - 'ísť' (to go)
rules.text: The verb 'ísť' uses irregular past participle 'išiel/išla/išlo/išli' in conditional.
examples: Išiel by som domov – I would go home (masc.)
Išla by som do školy – I would go to school (fem.)
Išli by sme na prechádzku – We would go for a walk

rules.title: Irregular Conditional - 'jesť' (to eat)
rules.text: The verb 'jesť' uses irregular past participle 'jedol/jedla/jedlo/jedli' in conditional.
examples: Jedol by som obed – I would eat lunch (masc.)
Jedla by som večeru – I would eat dinner (fem.)
Jedli by sme pizzu – We would eat pizza

rules.title: Irregular Conditional - 'piť' (to drink)
rules.text: The verb 'piť' uses irregular past participle 'pil/pila/pilo/pili' in conditional.
examples: Pil by som vodu – I would drink water (masc.)
Pila by som kávu – I would drink coffee (fem.)
Pili by sme čaj – We would drink tea

rules.title: Irregular Conditional - 'môcť' (can/to be able)
rules.text: The verb 'môcť' uses irregular past participle 'mohol/mohla/mohlo/mohli' in conditional.
examples: Mohol by som prísť – I could come (masc.)
Mohla by som pomôcť – I could help (fem.)
Mohli by sme zostať – We could stay

rules.title: Irregular Conditional - 'vedieť' (to know)
rules.text: The verb 'vedieť' uses past participle 'vedel/vedela/vedelo/vedeli' in conditional.
examples: Vedel by som odpoveď – I would know the answer (masc.)
Vedela by som pravdu – I would know the truth (fem.)
Vedeli by sme to – We would know it

rules.title: Common Irregular Verbs in Conditional
rules.text: Several common verbs have irregular past participles that must be memorized for conditional use.
examples: Išiel by som domov – I would go home
Jedol by som obed – I would eat lunch
Mohol by som pomôcť – I could help
Vedel by som to – I would know it
